title: Near future: 'ir a + infinitive'
objectives: Express plans and predictions with 'ir a + infinitivo'
Use it for the most common form of 'future' in everyday speech

vocab.es: voy a / vas a / va a / vamos a / vais a / van a
vocab.en: I'm going to, you're going to, etc.

grammar_notes: Pattern: 'ir' conjugated + 'a' + infinitive. Voy a comer = I'm going to eat.
This is by FAR the most common way to express the future in spoken Spanish — more common than the future tense itself.
Russian parallel: 'я собираюсь поесть' or 'я буду есть' — Spanish leans on the 'going to' shape.
example_phrases: Voy a estudiar esta tarde. (I'm going to study this afternoon.)
Mañana vamos a viajar a Barcelona.
¿Qué vas a hacer el fin de semana?
